seoTitle: How to Create a Bug in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to log a new bug in Jira with the right fields so your team can triage and fix it efficiently.
intro: A well-logged bug saves your team time by giving developers exactly what they need to reproduce and fix the issue. This guide walks through creating a bug in Jira and filling in the fields that matter most.

steps.heading: Open the create issue screen
steps.body: Click the create button and select Bug as the issue type from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Write a clear summary
steps.body: Summarize the problem in one line that describes what went wrong, not just the symptom.

steps.heading: Add steps to reproduce
steps.body: Describe the exact steps needed to trigger the bug so anyone can recreate it.

steps.heading: Note expected versus actual behavior
steps.body: State what should have happened and what actually happened so the gap is obvious.

steps.heading: Set priority and assign it
steps.body: Choose a priority level and assign the bug to the right person or team, then save it.

faq.question: What's the difference between a bug and a task in Jira?
faq.answer: A bug represents something broken or not working as expected, while a task is planned work that isn't necessarily tied to a defect.

faq.question: Should I attach screenshots to a bug report?
faq.answer: Yes, screenshots or screen recordings make it much easier for developers to understand and reproduce the issue.

faq.question: Can I link a bug to the story it affects?
faq.answer: Yes, bugs can be linked to related stories or epics so the impact is visible across the project.

faq.question: Who should set the bug's priority?
faq.answer: This varies by team, but it's often the reporter's initial guess, refined later by the assignee or product owner.
